name: Everolimus improved influenza-vaccine response in elderly volunteers
summary: RAD001/everolimus provides a human immune-function signal through influenza-vaccine response and T-cell exhaustion-marker movement.
statement: In an elderly-volunteer randomized trial, RAD001/everolimus enhanced influenza-vaccine response by about 20% at relatively well-tolerated doses and reduced the fraction of CD4 and CD8 T cells expressing PD-1.
time_horizon: Short-term treatment before influenza-vaccine response assessment
quantitative_note: The PubMed abstract reports about a 20% enhancement in influenza-vaccine response.
caveats: The endpoint was vaccine response and immune phenotype, not infection prevention, frailty, mortality, or broad healthy-aging benefit.
The trial tests a rapalog/TORC1 pharmacology branch, so it should not be generalized to all immune rejuvenation.
Long-term safety and durability for preventive older-adult use remain unresolved.
